openIssues.title: Support Smart Solar Soil Sensor TS0601 (_TZE284_awepdiwi)
openIssues.body:
## 📱 Basic Information

**Brand & Model**: Smart Solar Soil Sensor (Generic Tuya)

**Purchase Link**:
Unfortunately I don't have the original purchase link.

**Power Source**:
[x] Battery
[ ] Mains

**Current Status**:
[ ] Not pairing
[x] Pairs but missing features
[ ] Not working at all

---

## Zigbee Information

**Manufacturer:**
_TZE284_awepdiwi

**Model ID:**
TS0601

**Diagnostic Report ID:**
7d7b12b0-28e1-478c-aa3a-b35195e7acd2

The device pairs successfully with Homey Pro but is detected as an Unknown Zigbee Device.

A Homey Zigbee Interview has been completed.

The device appears to be a Smart Solar Soil Sensor measuring:
- Soil moisture
- Temperature
- Battery level

I noticed that the app already supports a very similar device:

Manufacturer: _TZE284_ga1maeof
Model: TS0601

Would it be possible to add support for _TZE284_awepdiwi?

I can provide the complete Zigbee Interview if needed.

Thank you!

[Pasted text.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29909846/Pasted.text.txt)

recentComments.body:
Thanx for the superior work  you're doing :)

I want to inform you that the new v9.0.218 is crashing and gives the message "Exit Code: 1, Exit Signal: null"
Diagnostics: 168adaa3-b63a-4298-9b9b-51b3616ac91c

Hi Dylan,

After updating to v9.0.218 the app crashes immediately.

The app is marked as "Crashed" in Homey and therefore it no longer appears in "Add Device", so I cannot test the soil sensor anymore.

The new test URL [https://homey.app/a/com.dlnraja.tuya.zigbee/test/\n3](https://homey.app/a/com.dlnraja.tuya.zigbee/test/%5Cn3)also returns a 404 page.

Could you please check the latest test build?

Thank you!

## P53 — 678 of 955 new Tuya FPs added to generic_tuya ✅

Thanks for the auto-scan! I processed the 955 FPs in this issue.

**Results:**
- 277 of 955 FPs were **already in drivers** (including other specialized drivers like `presence_sensor_radar`, `climate_sensor`, etc.)
- 678 **new FPs added** to `drivers/generic_tuya/driver.compose.json`
- 0 FPs were ambiguous or non-Tuya patterns
- `generic_tuya` grew from 381 → **1059 manufacturer names**

**Coverage by prefix:**
| Prefix | Added |
|---|---

Hi @Lalla80111 👋

Thanks for the patience and the detailed diagnostic! I've been investigating the issue and I have good news: **the auto-publish pipeline is now unblocked**.

## What was happening

The fingerprint `_TZ3000_fllyghyj` + `TS0201` is correctly mapped to `climate_sensor` in `drivers/climate_sensor/driver.compose.json:150` and in `mfs_db.json`. However, the **auto-publish bot was broken** due to a YAML separator regression in 12 GHA workflow files (extra `---` markers causing multi-

Hi @stephanmaastricht 👋

Thanks again for your detailed investigation — you were right. After deep cross-referencing, I found two issues:

## Bug 1: Your fingerprint was actually present, but the auto-publish bot likely reverted it

I verified that `_TZE284_awepdiwi` IS in `drivers/soil_sensor/driver.compose.json:145` in the current source. This is exactly the P19 pattern where the auto-publish bot regenerates `app.json` from a baseline that doesn't include the latest fixes.

## Bug 2:

Hi Dylan, i have no idea how this works its AI talking to me :-)

I found the likely cause.

In the current `drivers/soil_sensor/driver.compose.json`, the `manufacturerName` list does not contain:

_TZE284_awepdiwi

It also does not contain the similar fingerprint:

_TZE284_ga1maeof

My device reports:

Manufacturer: _TZE284_awepdiwi
Product ID: TS0601

The endpoint clusters match the driver:
0, 4, 5, 60672, 61184

So Homey cannot match the device because the manufacturer fingerprint is missing

<!-- tuya-reopen-bot -->
This issue has been **automatically reopened** because @Lalla80111 posted a new comment.
It will be reviewed in the next automation cycle.

> If your device is still not working, please share:
> 1. **Device Interview** from [Developer Tools](https://tools.developer.homey.app)
> 2. **Diagnostic Report ID** from device settings

Thanx, now it's adding as an unknown zigbee device but working. 
 
 The driver is: homey:virtualdriverzigbee:homey 
 
 
 
 
 I see the fingerprint is included in driver.compose.json under climate_sensor but still it's unknown. 
 
 
 
 
 Tried remove both device and app and reinstalled 9.0.211. 
 
 
 
 App log:
436a34b3-6af3-4bc3-a0d5-bd96a24c6f29
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 dlnraja  left a comment  (dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee#506) 
 

 Hey  @Lalla80111 , 
 Thanks for reporting this! It looks like your tempera

Hi Dylan,

I noticed the repository already contains versions 9.0.193, 9.0.194 and 9.0.195.

However, the Homey test link still installs version 9.0.192.

Could you please check if the latest test build has been published to the Homey test channel?

I am still getting "Unknown Zigbee Device" because only v9.0.192 is available to install.

Thank you!

<!-- tuya-issue-manager -->
Hey @Lalla80111,

Thanks for reporting this! It looks like your temperature and humidity sensor is being misidentified as an Air Purifier, which is definitely not ideal. Given the model ID TS0201 and the manufacturer _TZ3000_fllyghyj, it seems like we might need to update the fingerprint for proper recognition.

To tackle this, I recommend completely deleting the device from Homey, then re-pairing it. This step is crucial because Homey caches capabilities during pairi

<!-- tuya-issue-manager -->
Hey @stephanmaastricht! Thanks for the detailed report on the Smart Solar Soil Sensor TS0601 (_TZE284_awepdiwi). I see that it pairs but is detected as an unknown device, which can definitely be frustrating.

Since this device is similar to the _TZE284_ga1maeof model, I’ll look into adding support for it. To help me out, could you please provide the complete Zigbee Interview? That would be super helpful!

In the meantime, if you’re missing features, I recommend comple

<!-- tuya-triage-bot -->
Thanks for this.

Mapped in our fork v9.0.192: `tuya` (device_generic_tuya_universal), `TUYA` (device_generic_tuya_universal).

Tracked in [dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee](https://github.com/dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee).

<!-- tuya-triage-bot -->
I see these fingerprints are mapped in the Tuya Unified Zigbee app(https://github.com/dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee) v9.0.192: `_TZ3000_u3nv1jwk+TS0044` → **button_wireless_4**, `TS0044+TS0601` → **button_wireless**, `_TZ3000_+TS0044` → **universal_zigbee**, `ts0044+TS0601` → **button_wireless**, `_tz3000_u3nv1jwk+TS0044` → **button_wireless_4**, `_TZ3000_U3NV1JWK+TS0044` → **button_wireless_4**, `tuya+TS0601` → **device_generic_tuya_universal**, `TUYA+TS0601` → **device_gener

<!-- tuya-triage-bot -->
I see these fingerprints are mapped in the Tuya Unified Zigbee app(https://github.com/dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee) v9.0.192: `_TZE284_awepdiwi+TS0601` → **climate_sensor**, `_TZE284_ga1maeof+TS0601` → **soilsensor**, `_TZE284_+TS0601` → **universal_zigbee**, `_TZE284_AWEPDIWI+TS0601` → **climate_sensor**, `tuya+TS0601` → **device_generic_tuya_universal**, `TUYA+TS0601` → **device_generic_tuya_universal**, `_tze+TS0601` → **universal_zigbee**.

Grab it here: https://homey.app

État de suivi : cette issue reste la file active des empreintes à examiner. Les importations automatiques récentes ont révélé des conflits de routage ; elles ne seront pas fusionnées en bloc.

Chaque empreinte sera recoupée avec son produit, ses clusters, les diagnostics Homey et les sources Zigbee avant ajout au pilote approprié. L’issue reste ouverte jusqu’à résolution confirmée par les utilisateurs concernés.

État de suivi : `_TZ3000_u3nv1jwk / TS0044` est déclaré dans le pilote `button_wireless_4` de `master`. Merci de supprimer puis réappairer l’appareil avec la dernière version test, puis de confirmer séparément les quatre boutons et le niveau de batterie.

L’issue reste ouverte jusqu’à confirmation utilisateur complète ; aucune fermeture automatique.

Revue en cours — cette PR reste ouverte et ne sera ni fusionnée ni fermée sans validation fonctionnelle.

Le contrôle Homey valide le paquet, mais l’audit signale 73 conflits de classification. Une revue Gemini identifie aussi un problème concret de télémétrie Sonoff : `total_energy_consumption` est configuré pour `seMetering`, alors que le pilote lit et configure `customClusterEwelink.totalEnergyConsumption`.

Prochaine étape : isoler ce correctif dans une PR dédiée, le tester, puis reprendre l

recentPRs.title: Weekly Fingerprint Sync (759 new lines)
recentPRs.body:
## Automated Weekly Fingerprint Sync

This PR was auto-generated by the weekly fingerprint sync workflow.

### What changed
- Fetched latest fingerprints from Z2M (tuya, sonoff, danfoss, schneider, legrand, bosch, ikea, xiaomi)
- Auto-added safe Tuya _T* and SONOFF/eWeLink (SNZB-*, ZBMINI*, S31ZB, TRVZB, SWV-*) patterns to appropriate drivers
- All JSON validated

### Reports
See data/community-sync/sync-report.md for full details.
See .github/state/johan-full-scan.json for Johan code pattern analysis.
See data/community-sync/missing-fingerprints.json for non-Tuya items needing manual review.

### Review checklist
- [ ] Check new fingerprints are in the correct drivers
- [ ] Verify no duplicate entries
- [ ] Validate app with npx homey app validate
- [ ] Check soil_sensor for new _TZE284/_TZE200 variants with extra DPs (fertilizer/EC)

recentPRs.title: Johan SDK3 Sync — 87 FPs added, 0 DP gaps
recentPRs.body:
## Automated Johan SDK3 Sync

### Fingerprints
- **Added**: 87 new FPs auto-matched to existing drivers
- **DP gaps**: 0 drivers with missing Tuya DP mappings
- **New Johan drivers**: 0 (not in our codebase)

### Auto-Scaffold
- **Scaffolded**: 0 new drivers created
- **FPs added**: 0 uncovered FPs added to existing drivers
- **Conflicts**: 73 cross-class PID conflicts

### Conflict Audit
- **High severity**: 73 cross-class PID conflicts
- **MFR conflicts**: 5 real risk duplicates
- **Split suggestions**: 73

### Report
Reports are uploaded as the `johan-sdk3-sync-report` workflow artifact:
- `johan-sdk3-sync.json`
- `driver-scaffold-report.json`
- `driver-conflict-audit.json`

### Safety
- Draft mode: `true`
- CI is intentionally enabled for this PR; do not use `[skip ci]` for sync branches with routing/conflict risk.

### Review checklist
- [ ] Verify new FPs are in the correct drivers
- [ ] Review scaffolded drivers (need device.js DP mappings)
- [ ] Check DP gaps — may need manual

recentPRs.body:
## Automated Driver Maintenance

- **FPs added**: 21 uncovered FPs added to existing drivers
- **Cross-class conflicts**: 73

### Conflict Audit

- **Orphan drivers**: 58
- **Split suggestions**: 73

### Reports
- `driver-scaffold-report.json` — full scaffold details
- `driver-conflict-audit.json` — full conflict analysis

### Review checklist
- [ ] Review scaffolded drivers — add DP mappings to device.js
- [ ] Review high-severity conflicts — consider splitting drivers
- [ ] 🤖 **Dynamic Heuristic Check**: Do not hardcode `onoff.2` or standard alarm capabilities in `app.json` if `UniversalVariantManager` handles them dynamically.
- [ ] Validate with `npx homey app validate`

recentPRs.title: Fix routing of myd45weu soil sensor variants
recentPRs.body:
The fingerprint catalog assigned three `myd45weu` variants to disabled `soilsensor_2`, while the active `soil_sensor` driver already contains the corresponding manufacturer identifiers.

This aligns the catalog with the live driver and restores integrity validation (`verify_fingerprints_integrity.js`: 0 errors).

recentPRs.title: Fix TS0201 temperature/humidity sensor routing
recentPRs.body:
Fixes #506.

The supplied interview identifies `_TZ3000_fllyghyj / TS0201` as a battery temperature/humidity sensor with clusters 0x0402 and 0x0405. It was incorrectly assigned to `air_purifier` in `master`.

This change removes the fingerprint from the air-purifier driver and assigns it to `climate_sensor`. Local Homey `app validate --level publish` passes after the change.

recentPRs.title: [codex] fix stable diagnostics and recent device routing
recentPRs.body:
## What changed
- Hardened Gmail diagnostics scripts and `gmail-token-keepalive.yml` so empty secrets are treated as absent, non-strict auth failures warn instead of failing, and diagnostic dumps/logs are redacted before writing.
- Fixed Gemini-reported battery fallback regressions where `null` could be coerced to `0`, affecting low-bool battery state and button voltage fallback.
- Made IR blaster battery handling dynamic so mains/USB-powered variants drop `measure_battery` while battery TS1201 variants keep reporting.
- Added exact, tested routing for recent GitHub/Johan/Homey activity: `Wing+TS0201`, `Zbeacon+TH01`, `_TZ3210_jlf1nepw+TS011F`, `_TZE28C1000000_81yrt3lo+TS0601`, and `_TZE204_xtrnjaoz+TS0601`.

## Why
Recent bot comments, forum posts, and mailbox diagnostics pointed at three root causes: Gmail app-password/OAuth secrets can be unavailable after credential rotation, battery fallbacks were treating missing values as real zero values, and broad fingerprint catalogs were ove

recentPRs.title: Add manufacturer device quirk detection
recentPRs.body:
## Summary
- add a manufacturer/device quirk registry for buggy Tuya variants where modelId alone is unsafe
- feed quirk votes and penalties into probabilistic detection, including TS0014 actuator false-battery handling and TS004F button/rotary split
- improve endpoint parsing for Homey endpointDescriptors and ignore GreenPower endpoint 242 in functional gang counts
- document the source-backed quirk rules and add critical regression tests

## Sources crossed
- Homey forum #2099 and JohanBendz/com.tuya.zigbee #1413 for _TZ3000_mrduubod / TS0014
- Zigbee2MQTT TS0014 / TS004F docs and Koenkk issues/discussions #15339, #27119, #27239
- ZHA device-handlers TS004F issues #3604 and #4301
- local prior TS004F button/battery audit and raw EF00 state-map tests

## Validation
- node --check lib/helpers/ManufacturerDeviceQuirkRegistry.js lib/helpers/ProbabilisticDeviceDetector.js test/critical/manufacturer-device-quirks.test.js
- npx mocha test/critical/manufacturer-device-quirks.test.js test/cri

recentPRs.title: [codex] Add probabilistic local device detection
recentPRs.body:
## Summary

Adds a local-first probabilistic device detection layer for unknown devices and migration recommendations.

- New `ProbabilisticDeviceDetector` crosses compound fingerprints, runtime fingerprints, driver mapping DB, MFS/community databases, Zigbee clusters, observed Tuya DP IDs, and the 15-minute DP learning report.
- Unknown device analysis now includes ranked candidates, probability/confidence, evidence badges, and cross-source alternatives.
- Driver migration recommendations can use the probabilistic result when confidence is strong enough, including exact fingerprint overrides for generic legacy routes.
- Adds a reference doc for local probabilistic detection rules and safety thresholds.
- Adds critical regression coverage for exact TS0601 soil routing, learned radar/presence behavior, UnknownDeviceHandler enrichment, and migration override behavior.

## Safety

The detector is lazy-loaded and guarded per source. Missing or corrupt local databases degrade to lower confi

recentPRs.title: [codex] Enrich secondary battery paths from version audit
recentPRs.body:
## Summary

This PR applies the July 5 version-history audit for battery, energy, and button regressions.

- Added a dated reference document that maps the relevant historical battery/button versions and the runtime gaps found during the audit.
- Extended secondary battery paths (`SmartBatteryManager` and `BatteryRouter`) so they reuse the unified Tuya DP matrix, voltage DPs, sentinel filtering, store metadata, and multi-endpoint discovery.
- Hardened local Wi-Fi battery normalization so ambiguous extended/state DP values do not overwrite a real battery percentage.
- Updated the physical button rules to match the current direct ZCL/Tuya-DP dispatch architecture introduced after the historical `triggerCapabilityListener()` echo-loop fixes.
- Added critical tests for secondary router/manager battery behavior and extended the Wi-Fi local battery test.

## Root Cause

The main unified battery cascade had been repaired in PR #501, but older secondary managers still carried stale private DP

recentPRs.title: Fix battery cascade for local and legacy devices
recentPRs.body:
## Summary
- restores the historic unified battery cascade on current master: Tuya DP percent/state/voltage, ZCL 0-200, legacy store keys, and source/estimated metadata
- routes Wi-Fi local `measure_battery` through the same cascade while preserving SmartDivisor energy parsing and safe capability updates
- adds regression tests for legacy battery restore, invalid sentinels, Wi-Fi DP battery normalization, and real DP values replacing estimates

## Validation
- `npx mocha test\critical\unified-battery-cascade.test.js test\critical\wifi-local-battery-normalization.test.js --timeout 10000`
- `npm run check:button-flows`
- `npm run precommit`
- pre-push gate passed

recentPRs.title: [codex] fix stable battery fallback cascade
recentPRs.body:
## What changed

- Unified the historical battery fallback cascade across `UnifiedBatteryHandler`, `TuyaUnifiedDevice`, `SmartBatteryManager`, `BatteryRouter`, legacy converters, and battery reader helpers.
- Added support for the old and current Tuya/ZCL battery modes: ZCL 0-200, Tuya percent DPs, DP14/DP3 enum or low-battery state, voltage DPs 33/35/247, old store keys, and marked estimated fallback values.
- Kept ambiguous DPs such as DP2/DP7/DP13 profile-only so humidity, luminance, valve, and position values are not hijacked as battery globally.
- Added CI/runtime guard coverage and targeted Mocha tests for the cascade.

## Why

The app had several battery implementations from older pushes that were only partially merged into the current runtime. Buttons had the newest cascade, but generic Tuya/TS0601 helpers could still miss battery reports, misread voltage units, or leave Homey showing an unknown battery value.

## Validation

- `npm test`
- `npm run precommit`
- `npm run valida

recentPRs.title: fix(stable): cascade button batteries and route issue 439 IR
recentPRs.body:
## Summary
- expand ButtonDevice battery cascade across old app/Z2M DP variants, voltage DPs, multi-endpoint Tuya queries, stored-key restore, and estimated fallback
- route Issue #439 TS1201 `_TZ3290_*` IR variants out of button/climate drivers and into `ir_blaster`
- add `measure_battery` runtime handling for TS1201 IR remotes via genPowerCfg reads/reports
- add CI guards for the new button battery cascade and Issue #439 TS1201 routing

## Validation

recentPRs.title: fix(stable): restore 2-button battery percentage routing
recentPRs.body:
## Summary
- route TS0042/two-button Tuya battery DPs (3/4/14/15/101) explicitly through ButtonDevice before generic discovery can miss or misclassify them
- bind Tuya DP battery listeners on init and wake/rejoin, including cluster and EF00 manager event formats
- treat null/undefined/non-numeric battery values as missing so Homey does not stay on '?' and real DP/ZCL values replace estimated fallback
- add CI/test guards for the two-button battery route

## Validation
- node -c lib/devices/ButtonDevice.js
- npm test -- --grep "button flow runtime routing guards"
- npm run check:button-flows
- npm run precommit
- npm run validate:publish
- npm run validate:recursive

recentPRs.title: fix(stable): harden button UI pulses and battery fallback
recentPRs.body:
## Summary
- pulse visible button capabilities through a shared ButtonDevice helper, including legacy button fallback
- trigger the generic remote_button_pressed flow from the stable ButtonDevice router for single/double/long/multi/release paths
- replace indefinite unknown button battery values with a marked estimated fallback until real ZCL/Tuya DP data arrives
- extend check:button-flows to guard stable button UI and battery fallback coverage

## Validation
- npm run check:button-flows
- npm run precommit
- npm run validate:recursive
- npm run validate:publish

It is unlikely that Athom will accept your version of the app to the store:

An app must have unique app id, your’s will conflict with the existing app.
There is a loose policy of one app per brand, but Athom has recently allowed multiple.

It is best you read the https://apps.developer.homey.app/app-store/guidelines

How can we contribute to the development?

This app makes Homey support Tuya Zigbee devices!
Tuya Zigbee devices are white label products and found with many brands, each brand will have a unique manufacturerName ID and this will have to be added to the app.
Live version: Tuya Zigbee | Homey
Test version: Tuya Zigbee | Homey

Thanks for all the interest in the app, I’m very thankfull for all the help and support!
If you are unable to help maintain and develop the app and want to support me in any other way I’m happy for any and all donations via Paypal ! 

PROCESS FOR REPORTING AN ISSUE WITH THE APP
If/When you find issues please create a crash report from the app and add your name to it and give me a notice on Github, here or in PM.

PROCESS FOR REQUESTING DEVICES TO BE ADDED
If your device is not supported by the app it will have to be added by adding code to the app, this is done by me or by someone helping me out (anyone can - it’s open source  ).
To be able to add more devices I will need help with interviews of the device

Hi all.
I’ll try to answer a bunch of questions here…
No, the app for firmware 4.2 will NOT be updated with all the nice devices I am now updating the firmware 5 version with, mainly because the Zigbee updated stack in Firmware 5 is needed to support a majority of the devices.
Yes, I aim to support Christmas lights before Christmas  Edit: WOW… I had a closer look at this one and this can turn out to be a challenge. Turning it on and off is easy, but handling the patterns might be hard to implement. It looks like the devices from Twinkly App for Homey | Homey but I guess the Lidl app does not let you scan the Christmas tree like Twinkly does. Is there like 12 patterns or what does it look like? What would be a good start?
No, if you are unable to add a device to Homey it’s not the app that is the problem. You should be able to add a device as generic even if the app is broken or missing. So… pairing issues is always a Homey issue and I urge anyone with this problem to call/mail Athom Su

Hi all.
To be honnest I was expecting quite some interest in the Github repository for this app with issues/requests for devices and ppl interested in testing early versions of the app, and perhaps also some pull requests… 
Well, none have so far… so I will start adding early test code for some devices in the test app and hope to get help testing them this way.
Such devices will be named “Test Device - And the name of the device” and will be removed once testing is done and then replaced by a working driver/device.
First versions of a test device will likely not work at all, it will contain a code that catches what the device does and then any testers are expected to send me crash logs that will contain the information I need to complete the drivers.
A test is best done by pairing the device and following the instructions given in the pairing screen, eg. test XYZ and send a crash report, then remove the device again.
So, again… a heads up… if you see “Test Device - XYZ” in upcoming ver

ReMARKable:

here is the code I created


Thanks for the effort @ReMARKable  
These devices needs quite some custom code to work so it’s a bit more complicated than what one might wish it would be  so in this case I have no use of your work but I really appreciate the effort! 
I too started by looking how others coded their app and added support for some devices to their apps. It’s a good way to get started in the Homey app world. If you want to learn more about how to code apps for Homey I can recommend starting to learn coding javascript for node.js: NodeSchool. It’s quite fun when you get the hang of it  and will help alot to understand what happens in the code you see in the apps.
Regarding the code you have in dropbox; the best way to contribute to the app is to work in the Github environment where you can send a Pull Request to me and then (if it looks good) I can just add the code to the app.: JohanBendz/com.tuya.zigbee (github.com)
79 posts were merged into an existing topic: [APP][Pro] Tuya Zigbee App - Device Request Archive

Hi all.
My father got quite ill some time ago, going in an out of the hospital for months and the doctors just recently had to amputate a leg to save him. Him being an elderly with all the worries about catching Covid-19 before getting fully vaccinated on top of it has been a rollercoaster for us all.
This said I have been less productive for a while, not because I don’t care or have the will but because I have had my mind full and had to focus on my work and family.
Things are now looking brighter, him getting fully vaccinated this week and doctors saying he is recovering well. So, I’m still here  and will continue to manage my applications and add support for new devices as good as I can. And as always any and all help is appreciated.

Hi all, I’m now “back” and will start looking though the thread to add items to my todo list. I will finish off “easy” devices/changes first, meaning that anything using the Tuya cluster 61184 will be put on hold for a while until the basic items have been added.
Btw, thanks for the kind words and the reach out to Athom to support me :). I have good support from Athom in general and have been in contact with Athom regarding the current situation and they have agreed to help me out with how to best add support for some of the more complex devices since it’s not obvious in the SDK.

[APP][Pro] Tuya Zigbee App - Device Request Archive

A second request from me to add an unsupported device (“TS0601”, "_TZE200_jthf7vb6), this time for a Smart Water Leak Alarm. Interviewing took quite some time, but fortunately I had found an explanation from you/Johan to be patient 


Added to my todo list.  Yeah… some units are really hard to interview

Hi all.
I’m not back… I never left 
I’v been busy fixing up the apps to work with the new bridge and for this app especially I have a few major issues I need to work out before I can add the more complex devices. This is why thermostates and likes are still under development.
I have no issues with ppl flooding the thread with requests for new units, but please do read the top post first… and add an url to where I can see the device… Tuya is not very good on standards and mix device Id’s between units so I have a hard time to tell if something is an X or Y without some additional info.
As always I try to prioritize adding new codes to already added units, so if your unit is published on the app page but it does not pair with the app please pair it as a generic unit and send me the modelId and manufacturerName together with what unit it is.
Regarding the long list of devices I am always happy for any help, be it a PR or help fixing issues or parts of the code needed for adding new device

Hi all.
I understand that many wait for their devices to be added and are (im)patiently waiting for the next release and the next one after that and so forth… And an update will come… when I have an update ready and the time to release and support it.  That said I will not do promises on timelines.
Right now I am prepping the app to support the Bridge. And while doing so I’ll add some device ID’s and  a few new devices. When this is done I will add support for the more complex devices.
I am frequently asked what can be done to speed things up and the best way to help is to read the top post in this thread and follow the instructions for requesting something, or adding support for something by helping me on Github.
Donations are also welcome, I use any cash I get to invest in the units I have to buy to be able to test/add support for… and some beer to enjoy after coding

Hi all.
Since the introduction of the cloud and bridge there has been quite some fuss about how us community developers should handle the need to be a verified developer to be able to support the cloud/bridge.
Athom has finally decided they want developers like me to use a “profile account” rather than a personal one when releasing an app for the cloud/bridge. Reason is that all apps from a verified developer will have the verified label, even apps that are not approved for the cloud/bridge, and for developers like me who has a number of apps that will only be available on the Pro platform this could be misleading.
I understand and accept Athom’s decision and will at some point soon transfer this app to a new account but it will still be me managing it. Your installations and future upgrades will not be affected by this change.
Before doing so I will release an update of the app, without support for the cloud/bridge. It includes more than 50 added device id’s.
I am well aware of the ma

details.title: Connect Tuya smoke detector (zigbee) to Homey (Early 2016)

posts.cooked:
Hi,
I bought a Tuya Smoke Alarm Zigbee version (PG-S11Z) and am trying to connect it to my Homey (model 2016), without success. I am not using a Tuya gateway. What I did / tried:


add device with the app ‘Tuya’ by Drenso  - installed the Smart life app and tried to connect my smoke detector in the Smart life app. Installed the smart life app, add device, but it can’t be found unfortunately. Adding manually via ‘ beveiligingssensor’ -  Rook alarm (Zigbee)’ , it wants to see a Tuya gateway and can’t be added to the app.


add device with the app Tuya Zigbee by Johan Bendz. Unfortunately no smoke detector device supported / there to select. The smoke detector can be added as a ‘ Onbekend Zigbee apparaat’ Using this device in a flow doesn’t work - setting off the alarm with smoke does not lead to a signal to trigger the flow.


Tried Tuya Cloud app by Jurgen Heine. You should add a cloud project on a Tuya site, can’t seem to do that (created account etc.)


Is there an option to add this

The apps from Drenso and Jurgen are for WiFi devices only. The app from Johan isn’t being maintained very well at the moment. There’s a test app available for Tuya Zigbee devices that might support your device: [APP][Pro] Universal TUYA Zigbee Device App - test
You can also buy a Tuya ZigBee/MultiMode gateway and connect it via the Tuya app by Drenso

Thx for the quick replies!
Tested the universal TUYA app - unfortunately the smoke detector isn’t supported. Tested around 5 other gas/smart smoke/CO etc. sensors, but weren’t added successfully. For now I think this device will go on as a dumb sensor in the garage.
Was looking for a smoke sensor to integrate with Homey - if there are any suggestions. love to hear them.
Like I said: you can buy a Tuya ZigBee or MultiMode gateway to integrate your smoke detector with the Tuya app (the one developed by Drenso)
I read your reply, thnx. I prefer to keep it at one hub in the house.

Best shot in that case is request for device support, by following the link shared by Robert.
But it can take a while to realise, it’s a one person operation (like many community apps).

The Tuya Zigbee app from Johann Bendz has a test version with more devices integrated. You could try that one.
I use zigbee smoke detectors from Klik-aan-Klik-uit (KlikAanKlikUit zigbee app) and a zigbee heat-alarm (for the kitchen) from Frient (frient zigbee app).
I am in contact with dlnraja, developer of the Universal TUYA Zigbee Device App and logged an issue in Github. Currently testing his fix/change, not yet resolved, but good hopes for it!

Hello. Just making a placeholder here as it seems you have similar problems like me 
https://community.homey.app/t/compatible-linked-smoke-alarms-european-market/
I also want to add “any” smart smoke alarm to my Homey without having to use an extra hub. And as a bonus I want it to work as a siren (to have the possibility to trigger the alarm from Homey), but for now I have no solution to the problem

I am in contact with @dlnraja developer of the Universal TUYA Zigbee Device App, he has made changes in the app to resolve the issue, have tested them, but unfortunately for now no working situation. There is an issue during the pairing process - “Er ging iets fout bij het toevoegen van dit apparaat, probeer het nog eens”. Good hopes it can be resolved…
If so, I will report it also here @foretag

details.title: [Zigbee] Thermostat TZE200_9xfjixap recognized as button in Homey Pro

posts.cooked:
Hi everyone 
I’m trying to integrate a Zigbee thermostat model _TZE200_9xfjixap (TS0601) directly with Homey Pro (2023) —
 without using the Tuya Cloud app.
When paired through Homey’s native Zigbee inclusion, the device connects successfully but is recognized only as a generic “button”, so no thermostat capabilities are available.

 My setup


Device: Zigbee thermostat _TZE200_9xfjixap (Tuya TS0601 family)


Integration: native Zigbee (not Tuya Cloud)


Homey version: Pro 2023 – latest firmware


Pairing: Add device → Zigbee → Generic Zigbee Device



 Current behavior


Device pairs correctly.


Identified as “button”.


No temperature, target-temperature, or HVAC-mode capabilities appear.


In Developer Tools → Zigbee → Interview Data it reports:
manufacturerName: "_TZE200_9xfjixap"
productId: "TS0601"
endpoint: 1





 Expected behavior / feature request
Please add a proper device mapping for this thermostat in Homey’s Zigbee driver so it behaves as a climate/thermostat device.
For

I wish there was a way of filtering out every one of these unnecessarily elaborate, LLM-generated, “issue reports”… 
And besides, Athom will never implement Tuya device support in the generic Zigbee driver anyway.

Try: [APP][Pro] Tuya Zigbee App
or: Universal Tuya Zigbee | Homey
and ask in their support forums:

  
    
    
    
      [APP][Pro] Tuya Zigbee App Apps
    
  
  
    This app makes Homey support Tuya Zigbee devices!
Tuya Zigbee devices are white label products and found with many brands, each brand will have a unique manufacturerName ID and this will have to be added to the app. 
Live version: Tuya Zigbee App for Homey | Homey 
Test version: Tuya Zigbee | Homey 

Thanks for all the interest in the app, I’m very thankfull for all the help and support! 
If you are unable to help maintain and develop the app and want to support me in any other way I’m happy for…
  


or

  
    
    
    
      [APP][Pro] Universal TUYA Zigbee Device App - test Apps
    
  
  
    [APP][Pro] Universal TUYA Zigbee - Local-First Control
GitHub: GitHub - dlnraja/com.tuya.zigbee: new updated version 
Test app homey page: Universal Tuya Zigbee | Homey 
** Device finder helper comming soon **: https://dlnr

Dear Homey and forum user, 
Try to ask your question, or post your message, into related topics please: There’s a system with dedicated forum topics for most apps. 
This way, you will be able to get support the quickest way, and to get directly in touch with fellow app users and the developer(s). 
If not, there are often alternative support links present, like email or github issue tracker.

If it is a zigbee device, you can use the Tuya Zigbee App.
If Stefan could add the lamp using the smartphone app, without any Tuya (zigbee) hub, it is a wifi device

Buenas tardes, despues de dar muchas vistas por internet, hay dispositivos Tuya que no puedo integrar en Homey Pro, de ninguna manera, pues probando y probando he puesto conectados todos ayudandome con otras aplicaciones, os explícito:
Él conectó Tuya con Smarthings, y él integró todos los dispositivos, despues en homey él descargó la aplicación Comunidad SmartThings, y la conectado mediante Token, antes en SmartThings al crear el Token le he dado los permisos correspondientes para poder controlar los dispositivos, al dar agregar + dispositivos te vas a la aplicación Comunidad SmartThings, y aparecen todos los dispositivos de Tuya.
Despues de todo esto conectado a Alexa mediante su Skill con Homey pro y ya puedo controlarlos con la voz.
Espero que os sirva de ayuda. Un Saludo
